description_long: This object is a flintlock-style shotgun with a dark wooden stock and a metal barrel, reminiscent of historical firearms from the 18th or early 19th century. The barrel is relatively short and cylindrical, with a flared muzzle and metallic finish, while the lock mechanism features a prominent hammer and frizzen assembly. The stock is smoothly contoured, extending into a rounded grip that fits comfortably in the hand. The trigger guard is rounded and metallic, encasing a single trigger beneath the action. The overall construction combines wood and metal, with visible screws and mechanical details on the lock and trigger area. The finish is matte to semi-gloss, with the wood showing a rich brown color and the metal parts appearing dark gray to black.
description: A historical flintlock shotgun with a dark wooden stock, short metal barrel, and prominent hammer mechanism. The design features a rounded grip and a metallic trigger guard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle opening and the alignment of the barrel, with the hammer and frizzen assembly visible above.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the barrel, the wooden stock, and the detailed lock mechanism with hammer and frizzen.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rounded end of the wooden stock and the top of the lock mechanism, with the trigger guard partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the barrel, stock, and lock mechanism from the other side, with similar features as view 1.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, short, cylindrical with a flared muzzle and no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized metallic guard with a smooth contour and no finger grooves
